name: D+Q AD-risk cognition and mobility exploratory result
comparator: Single-arm before-versus-after pilot without a control group
arm_details: Dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1250 mg for 2 days every 2 weeks over 12 weeks.

effect.raw_text: Mean MoCA increased by 1.0 point (95% CI -0.7, 2.7) overall; among participants with the lowest baseline MoCA scores, MoCA increased by 2.0 points (95% CI 0.1, 4.0). Mean TNF-alpha changed by -3.0% (95% CI -13.0, 7.1), and TNF-alpha change correlated inversely with MoCA change (r=-0.65, p=0.02).

analysis.method: Single-arm absolute and percent change estimates reported in PubMed abstract
analysis.effect_note: Overall MoCA confidence interval crosses zero; subgroup and biomarker-correlation results are exploratory.

statement: The AD-risk pilot reported an imprecise overall MoCA increase, a larger exploratory increase among participants with lower baseline MoCA scores, and an inverse TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation after 12 weeks of intermittent D+Q.
caveats: Single-arm N=12 completion sample without a control group.
Mobility endpoint values are not extracted from the abstract and need full article extraction.
Subgroup and biomarker-correlation signals are exploratory.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 40010154 abstract FINDINGS and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract supplies exact MoCA mean change and confidence interval, baseline-MoCA subgroup estimate, TNF-alpha percent change, and TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation.

name: D+Q postmenopausal bone trial mixed biomarker result

effect.raw_text: The study should be represented as mixed pending outcome-level extraction of primary endpoint, subgroup, and biomarker results.

statement: This aggregate triage result is retained only as a summary pointer; source-located extracted results now separate CTx, P1NP timepoints, SASP, BMD, and adverse-event outcomes.
caveats: Subgroup findings should remain labeled exploratory unless prespecified status is verified.

provenance.note: Triage-level aggregate pointer; do not use for synthesis when exact registry_extracted result records are available.

name: D+Q diabetic kidney disease senescence biomarker result
arm_details: Oral dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1000 mg for 3 days.

effect.raw_text: Abstract reports reduced adipose tissue senescent-cell burden within 11 days, including p16INK4A- and p21CIP1-expressing cells, senescence-associated beta-galactosidase activity, adipocyte progenitors with limited replicative potential, adipose macrophage/crown-like structure markers, skin p16INK4A and p21CIP1 cells, and circulating IL-1alpha, IL-6, MMP-9, and MMP-12; numeric effect sizes are not provided in the abstract.

statement: In an open-label N=9 diabetic kidney disease pilot, a 3-day D+Q course was followed by lower senescent-cell and SASP-related biomarker signals 11 days after treatment completion.
time_horizon: Before treatment to 11 days after completing a 3-day D+Q course
caveats: Open-label N=9 biomarker study; do not interpret as clinical efficacy.
Abstract-level extraction does not provide numeric effect sizes or p values for individual biomarkers.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 31542391 abstract METHODS, FINDINGS, and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract gives N=9, D+Q dose and timing, the 11-day post-treatment assessment, and directional biomarker decreases; it does not provide table-level numeric effects.

name: D+Q IPF randomized pilot dosing-completion and assessment-completion result
comparator: Matching placebo in a 1:1 randomized pilot
arm_details: Dasatinib 100 mg/day plus quercetin 1250 mg/day for 3 consecutive days per week over 3 weeks versus matching placebo.

effect.raw_text: All participants completed scheduled dosing (108/108 doses) and planned assessments (60/60). The D+Q arm had more non-serious adverse events than placebo (65 vs 22), no serious adverse events related to D+Q, sleep disturbances and anxiety were 4/6 vs 0/6, and explored frailty, pulmonary, or physical-function measures did not appear to differ meaningfully between groups.

analysis.effect_note: Completion counts were exact; clinical measures were underpowered and described qualitatively in the abstract.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 36857968 abstract FINDINGS and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract supplies exact dose-completion and assessment-completion counts plus qualitative between-arm interpretation of explored clinical measures.

statement: In the N=12 randomized IPF pilot, all scheduled doses and planned assessments were completed, while clinical measures were underpowered and did not appear to differ meaningfully between D+Q and placebo.
time_horizon: 3-week intervention period and planned pilot assessments
caveats: Small pilot trial not powered for efficacy.
The legacy generic IPF outcome/result records still need full reconciliation against article tables.
Clinical measures should remain separate from general aging or healthspan claims.

name: D+Q IPF feasibility and tolerability mixed result
comparator: Placebo control in a small randomized pilot

effect.raw_text: The pilot primarily informs feasibility, tolerability, and safety-boundary interpretation rather than definitive efficacy.

statement: The IPF randomized pilot should be used as safety and feasibility evidence unless later extraction verifies a powered efficacy outcome.
caveats: Disease-specific IPF outcomes do not establish general longevity benefit.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.

name: D+Q old mouse post-treatment survival positive result

effect.raw_text: Reported increased post-treatment survival in old mice after intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin.

statement: Preclinical survival evidence supports D+Q as an animal healthspan/lifespan signal, not as human aging efficacy.
caveats: Requires separate quantitative extraction before effect-size synthesis.
Do not pool with human clinical endpoints.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.

name: SToMP-AD no posted results
statement: NCT04685590 is a trial-watch record with no posted result at the checked registry update, so it contributes no effect evidence.
caveats: Requires surveillance for posted results or linked publication.

provenance.note: No posted results at checked registry update; not an extracted treatment effect.

name: Fisetin vascular trial no posted results
statement: NCT06133634 is a trial-watch record with no posted result at the checked registry update, so it contributes no effect evidence.
caveats: Requires surveillance for posted results or linked publication.

provenance.note: No posted results at checked registry update; not an extracted treatment effect.
